title: Energizing Algerian Chocolate Pudding
description: A funky, glassy-jelly dessert that combines dark chocolate chips with agar agar and a wood-fired bake for a satvik-friendly treat.
instructions: Melt the chocolate chips in a double boiler over low heat until smooth.
Whisk in the agar agar powder until fully dissolved.
Add almond milk, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and pinch of salt; stir continuously for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, stir in coconut oil until glossy.
Pour the mixture into a prepared baking dish and bake in a preheated wood-fired oven at 200°C (392°F) for 15 minutes.
Allow the pudding to cool to room temperature, then chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Once set, cut into squares or spoon into glassy portions.
Garnish with toasted almond slivers and optional diced fruit.
Serve chilled as a refreshing dessert.
